free_text: Elastic and inelastic scattering of vector polarized
deuterons by Fe and Ni Isotopes
The purely vector-polarized deutean beam.
The beam polarization itn was monitored continuously
in a polarimeter employing the 12C(d,p)13Cg.s.
reaction whose analysing power is well known:
Griffith,etc. Nucl.Phys. A185,49,1972.
Six silicon surface-barrier dE-E
telescopes with logarithmic particle-identifier  The
circuits. The detectors were mounted in two arrays of
three telescopes each placed symmetrically on opposite
sides of the incident beam direction.
Digitizing error

SD: DATA-CM corrected on DATA in Subents:
The target was self-supporting separated isotope
foil, 3 mg/cm thick.
Overall normalization uncertainty
In order to determine absolute cross sections, the
transmitted beam was collected in a Faraday cup.

from fig. 10
The target was self-supporting separated isotope
foil, 3 mg/cm thick.
The vector analysing power for elastic
scattering was measured with a statistical uncertainty
of +-0.02 or less except for +-0.03 at the largest
angles, enabling observation of small changes in the
data for the different targets.

The target was self-supporting separated isotope
foil, 3 mg/cm thick.
The statistical uncertainty of the inelastic data
points thus varies according to the ratio of the
inelastic to the elastic cross section.
